expectations.text: A handoff markdown file was created in a thoughts/handoffs/ or thoughts/shared/handoffs/ directory
expectations.evidence: File created at thoughts/handoffs/ via init-handoff.sh

expectations.text: The handoff file has valid YAML frontmatter containing at least date and branch fields
expectations.evidence: Frontmatter has 6 fields: date, git_commit, branch, repository, summary

expectations.text: The handoff document is under 200 lines total
The Tasks section describes the debugging investigation and its status (root cause found, fix not implemented)
expectations.evidence: Lines 11-14: describes investigation, 'Root cause confirmed, fix options identified, awaiting implementation'

expectations.text: The Learnings section captures the deadlock root cause: that the previous mutex fix was reverted because Dispatch() RLock conflicts with handler Lock
expectations.evidence: Line 27: 'Dispatch() holds RLock, and if handler tries Lock on same mutex chain, deadlock occurs'

expectations.text: The document references thoughts/debug-notes.md as prior investigation notes
expectations.evidence: Line 17: 'thoughts/debug-notes.md: Complete investigation findings including root cause and fix options'

expectations.text: The document mentions the three fix options (sync.Map, channel, restructure locking)
Action items specify implementing one of the fix approaches, not just 'continue debugging'
expectations.evidence: Line 35: 'Recommend starting with sync.Map (simplest, lowest risk)' with verification command

expectations.text: The document does not contain code blocks longer than 10 lines
The response to the user includes the /resume-handoff command with the handoff file path
expectations.evidence: Agent responded with '/resume-handoff' and file path

eval_feedback.overall: Clean pass. 40 lines is impressively concise while capturing all critical debugging context.
